author: agnxsh
content:
What is the maximum time for which clients should retain inclusion list say for example, in a very forked network? trying to understand these semantics so that inclusion list can be dealt with differently while syncing since I do notice, there are fork choice changes in heze.

cc <@845691100072509460>

author: agnxsh
content:
From <@654267572107083777> 

> because various answers to this question determine whether the ilpool itself should be storing all of this (if it's short-enough duration, yeah) or whether it's going to need database support (which, while I find the, say, store 18 days or something of ILs a profoundly uncompelling tradeoff in general, it would not make sense to have up to 8GB of ILs in memory

author: jih2nn
content: The CL needs to store ILs for the recent 2 slots whereas the EL needs to retain ILs for the tip of each branch. Validators will follow attestations for previous slots.

author: tersec
content: can this be somehow attached (directly or indirectly) to https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/v1.7.0-alpha.12/specs/heze/p2p-interface.md#inclusionlistbycommitteeindices-v1 ?

author: jih2nn
content: Please give it a review, if you have some time

author: jih2nn
content: Also, I'd like to mention that I'm planning to revamp IL store spec. It has quite a room for improvement IMO.
